title: payment — subprocessors
description: The Payment module engages SIX third-party subprocessors — one per provider driver. Every subprocessor receives payment identifiers (invoice ref + amount + currency) + customer identifiers (name, email, phone, billing address) + tokenized card references. GDPR Art. 28 DPA references + Art. 30 records of processing activities. Every tenant that activates a provider effectively adds that subprocessor to their processing register; the tenant admin acknowledges the DPA reference before configuring.

subprocessors.purpose: Payment processing — Stripe Payments (accept + capture) + Stripe Billing (subscription + invoice) + Stripe Radar (fraud detection) + 3D Secure 2 authentication + Stripe Connect (marketplaces).
subprocessors.data_hashing: Stripe hashes card PAN client-side via Stripe Elements; we NEVER see the PAN. Device fingerprint SHA-256'd before egress.
subprocessors.activation_gate: tenant admin acknowledges the DPA before saving the provider config

subprocessors.purpose: Merchant of Record payment processing — Paddle Classic (checkout overlay) + Paddle Billing v2 (subscription + invoice). Paddle is the MoR (they collect taxes, handle chargebacks, remit VAT to authorities).
subprocessors.data_hashing: Paddle handles PCI-DSS; we NEVER see the PAN.
subprocessors.activation_gate: tenant admin acknowledges DPA + confirms MoR relationship (Paddle becomes the seller-of-record for tax purposes)

subprocessors.purpose: Global payment processing — Adyen Checkout (accept + capture) + Adyen Marketplace (multi-party splits + sub-merchants) + Adyen Risk (fraud) + 3DS2. Adyen is the acquirer + processor (not MoR).
subprocessors.data_hashing: Adyen handles PCI-DSS via Adyen.js client-side tokenization; we NEVER see the PAN.
subprocessors.activation_gate: tenant admin acknowledges DPA. Marketplaces flow requires additional Adyen KYC on sub-merchants.

subprocessors.purpose: Payment processing — Square Payments + Cards API + Cash App Pay + Square Terminal (POS integration). US-heavy footprint.
subprocessors.data_hashing: Square handles PCI-DSS via Square Web Payments SDK client-side; we NEVER see the PAN.
subprocessors.activation_gate: tenant admin acknowledges DPA. Cash App Pay is US-only + requires additional Cash App Terms acknowledgement.

subprocessors.purpose: Payment processing — PayPal Checkout (accept via PayPal balance + card via Braintree) + Braintree (card acquiring) + Venmo (US consumer wallet).
subprocessors.data_hashing: PayPal handles PCI-DSS via Braintree client-side tokenization; we NEVER see the PAN.
subprocessors.activation_gate: tenant admin acknowledges DPA. Venmo is US-consumer-only.

subprocessors.purpose: Generic HMAC-signed JSON webhook. Escape hatch for tenants using their own payment gateway (Braintree direct, dLocal, PayFast, etc.). Data ultimately delivered to a caller-configured URL. Enterprise-only.
data_categories: full payment intent payload — cardholder_name, email, phone, billing_address, amount, currency, invoice_reference
subprocessors.data_hashing: none by Academorix — caller's endpoint responsible for handling. Caller MUST NOT accept raw PAN over this webhook (PCI-DSS scope violation).
subprocessors.dpa_url: N/A — the tenant is responsible for their downstream endpoint's DPA if any
certifications: depends on caller — tenant admin attests to PCI-DSS Level 1 compliance
subprocessors.activation_gate: Enterprise tenant admin confirms webhook URL + acknowledges DPA responsibility + attests to PCI-DSS Level 1 compliance on the receiving endpoint + explicit compliance-review workflow (manual security approval required)

downstream_subprocessors_referenced.used_for: Dispatching PaymentSucceededNotification / PaymentFailedNotification / PaymentActionRequiredNotification / PaymentMethodExpiringNotification / PaymentMethodAttachedNotification / PaymentDisputeOpenedNotification / PaymentDisputeEvidenceDueNotification / PaymentDisputeWonNotification / PaymentDisputeLostNotification / PaymentProviderCircuitBreakerOpenedNotification / PaymentReconcileDriftNotification via mail / SMS / push channels.

downstream_subprocessors_referenced.reference: The invoice module is the parent — every Payment references its parent invoice. Invoice PDF renderer + email sender are invoice-module concerns.
The transaction module receives PaymentCaptured events + records the double-entry ledger movement. transaction::subprocessors.json for downstream ledger sub-processing.

compliance::encryption owns the encryption service used for at-rest encryption of payment_methods.provider_reference_id + payment_intents.provider_reference_id + payment_intents.client_secret + payments.provider_metadata + payment_disputes.evidence.

growth::marketing subscribes to PaymentCaptured events for attribution. Marketing signals dispatch to a further 9 sub-processors (see growth::marketing::subprocessors.json) — separate consent gate applies (marketing + advertising consent categories).

notes: This file exists deliberately with 6 active subprocessors. Every tenant that configures a payment provider effectively activates that subprocessor for their tenant. The activation flow prompts the tenant admin to acknowledge the DPA reference before saving.
The tenant-facing subprocessor list (compliance/subprocessors surface) reads from this file + shows only the active subset per tenant (based on which providers the tenant has configured).
Adding a new provider driver = adding a new entry here + updating the tenant-facing subprocessor list + updating compliance.json Art. 28 evidence + updating the provider capability matrix.

CustomWebhook has the most restricted activation gate — Enterprise-only, manual security review, PCI-DSS attestation required. This is deliberate: caller-hosted gateways bypass the vendor-driven PCI-DSS scope reduction that the 5 first-party providers guarantee.
